headline: DRYDEN GOLD Hits 252 g/t Shoot Within 32.87 g/t Zone — Three New High-Grade Discoveries At Gold Rock, Ontario
snippet: Dryden Gold drilled 4.25m of 32.87 g/t gold including a 0.50m hit of 252 g/t at the new Sparrow zone, 300m northeast of existing resources at Gold Rock — a second hole hit Ruby for 4.0m of 6.51 g/t.

headline: SILVER ROCKETS 7% to $77 — China Imports 790+ Tonnes in Early 2026, Sixth Straight Annual Supply Deficit Looms
snippet: Silver surged nearly 7% to $77/oz on April 8, its highest since March 18, as China imports 790+ tonnes driven by solar demand. The global silver market is forecast to record its sixth consecutive structural supply deficit in 2026.

headline: ZIJIN PAYS C$5.5 Billion for Allied Gold — Chinese Miner Vaults to World's 6th Largest Gold Producer
snippet: Zijin Mining agreed to buy Allied Gold at C$44/share — a 27% premium — adding three producing African mines in Mali, Côte d'Ivoire, and Ethiopia. The C$5.5B deal is the largest gold M&A transaction of 2026, expected to close by late April.

headline: Founders Metals Drills 17.22 g/t Au at New Antino North Zone — Gold Fields Lifts Stake to 12.5%
snippet: Founders Metals made its first discovery at Antino North in Suriname, drilling 17.22 g/t Au over 3.6m within 24.6m of 2.81 g/t — the first hole testing one of twelve parallel northwest-trending shear zones mapped over multiple kilometres.

headline: NexGen and Denison BOTH Cleared to Build — First New Canadian Uranium Mines in Over 20 Years
snippet: NexGen Energy received its CNSC construction licence in March 2026 for Rook I (Canada's largest development-stage uranium deposit), while Denison's Phoenix ISR mine broke ground the same month — a combined $2.8B investment creating 1,600 construction jobs.

headline: GOLD RETREATS to $4,743 From $4,850 High — US-Iran Ceasefire Triggers Dollar Whiplash, Median Forecast $4,746
snippet: Gold fell $59 to $4,743/oz on April 9 after briefly surging above $4,850 on ceasefire news that reversed the dollar and crashed oil below $100. The metal still trades $1,568 higher than one year ago; a Reuters poll of 30 analysts pegs the 2026 median at $4,746.

headline: COPPER BREAKS $13,000/Tonne on Tariff Panic — But LME Inventories Hit 8-Year High in Contradictory Signal
snippet: Copper hit $5.87/lb ($13,000+/t) on April 10, up 2.1% on the day, as tariff fears and tight supply drove buyers forward — but LME warehouse stockpiles simultaneously climbed to an 8-year high, signalling subdued physical demand conditions.

headline: Core Lithium Restarts Finniss With $205M Glencore-Backed Package — First Spodumene Concentrate Targeted Q3 2026
snippet: Core Lithium's board approved the Finniss FID backed by a $70M Glencore/InfraVia convertible note, a $50M Nebari debt facility, and a A$120M equity raise. The project carries an NPV of $1.1B and targets 1.2Mtpa throughput by mid-2028.

headline: Mining M&A Hits 13-Year High at $93.7B — Mega-Deals Surge 68% With Gold Grabbing 77% of Total Volume
snippet: Global mining M&A hit $93.7B in 2025 — the highest since 2012 — with deals over $1B surging 68% year-over-year. Gold and silver assets attracted 77% of total deal flow; Coeur Mining completed its acquisition of New Gold in March 2026.

headline: GOLD MINERS Post Record Margins While Shares Still Lag The Metal — Historical Catch-Up Could Dwarf Bullion Gains
snippet: Gold equities are generating record free cash flow yet trail the metal's performance — mirroring the 2000-2011 bull market setup when miners ultimately returned 700% vs. gold's 500%. Analysts cite institutional underweight positions and compressed multiples as the opportunity.

headline: Uranium Cools to $84/lb After January's $101 Peak — Middle East War Creating Athabasca Basin Re-Pricing Event
snippet: Uranium spot peaked at $101.41/lb on January 29 before the outbreak of Middle East conflict triggered a swift retreat to $84.55 by early April — a 16% pullback that some analysts view as a buying opportunity given the AI-driven power demand surge.

headline: China AXES Li-Ion Battery Export Rebates From April 1 — Lithium Prices Surge as Two-Year Supply Glut Begins to Ease
snippet: China's removal of VAT export rebates on lithium-ion batteries effective April 1, 2026, has accelerated a lithium price recovery already underway since mid-December. Energy storage demand is now reinforcing the EV sector's continued growth in lithium off-take.
